items.title: One-dimensional arrays: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this C misconception? An array argument tells the called function its length automatically because the bracket size remains part of the parameter.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. Array parameters adjust to pointer parameters; pass the element count separately and enforce every index against that count.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. Array parameters adjust to pointer parameters; pass the element count separately and enforce every index against that count.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned C correction instead of the documented misconception.

options.text: Array parameters adjust to pointer parameters; pass the element count separately and enforce every index against that count.
An array argument tells the called function its length automatically because the bracket size remains part of the parameter.

answer.explanation: Array parameters adjust to pointer parameters; pass the element count separately and enforce every index against that count.

items.title: One-dimensional arrays: executable application
items.prompt: Read a count from one to 100 followed by that many integers, traverse the C array within bounds, and print only the greatest value.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent human standards, security, and assessment review.

hints: Start from input validation and the declared observable output, then apply only the target language rule before considering optimization.

feedback.correct: All visible and hidden deterministic behavior checks passed for the bounded source task.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic behavior check failed; inspect bounds, input state, and the target rule without exposing hidden cases.

criteria.description: Compiles in the pinned runtime and passes every normal and boundary behavior test.
Applies One-dimensional arrays without undefined behavior or an out-of-scope workaround.
